message:
fix(fp-io): schema validation error wording (br-597l / fd90.56)

Self-review of fd90.55 surfaced a pre-existing UX defect: every
schema-qualified SQL builder (sql_select_all_query_in_schema,
sql_select_columns_query_in_schema, sql_create_table_query_in_schema,
sql_insert_rows_query_in_schema, sql_drop_table_query_in_schema,
sql_multi_row_insert_query_in_schema, the SqlConnection trait's
schema-qualified table_exists override) validated the schema arg by
calling validate_sql_table_name(s). The shared identifier rule was
correct, but the error message said 'invalid table name: schemaXyz'
when actually the SCHEMA name was invalid — misleading users
debugging multi-schema SQL.

Fix is one-line per call site now that fd90.55 has the kind-
parameterized validate_sql_ident helper: add validate_sql_schema_name
thin wrapper that calls validate_sql_ident(s, schema). Bulk-replace
all 7 'validate_sql_table_name(s)?' call sites (where `s` is the
schema arg) with 'validate_sql_schema_name(s)?' via python script.

Update sql_select_all_query_in_schema_validates_schema_name and
sql_create_table_query_in_schema_validates_schema_name tests to
assert the new error wording 'invalid schema name'.

429 fp-io tests pass under default features; 246 under
--no-default-features; clippy -D warnings clean both profiles;
ubs Critical=0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
